books.title: Clean Code
books.author: Robert C. Martin
books.description: A handbook of agile software craftsmanship that teaches programmers how to write better code through meaningful names, clean functions, and proper formatting.

books.title: The Pragmatic Programmer
books.author: David Thomas & Andrew Hunt
books.description: From journeyman to master. This book examines the core process of software development and delivers practical advice to help you become a better programmer.

books.title: JavaScript: The Good Parts
books.author: Douglas Crockford
books.description: Most programming languages contain good and bad parts, but JavaScript has more than its share of the bad. This book scrapes away these bad features to reveal a subset of JavaScript that is reliable and readable.

books.title: Atomic Habits
books.author: James Clear
books.description: An easy and proven way to build good habits and break bad ones. Tiny changes, remarkable results. Learn how small behavioral shifts can lead to extraordinary outcomes.

books.title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
books.author: Daniel Kahneman
books.description: A groundbreaking exploration of the two systems that drive the way we think. System 1 is fast, intuitive, and emotional; System 2 is slower, more deliberative, and more logical.

books.title: The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People
books.author: Stephen R. Covey
books.description: A principle-centered approach for solving personal and professional problems. Covey presents a holistic approach to life that is rooted in timeless principles of fairness, integrity, and human dignity.

books.title: To Kill a Mockingbird
books.author: Harper Lee
books.description: The unforgettable novel of a childhood in a sleepy Southern town and the crisis of conscience that rocked it. Through the eyes of Scout Finch, a tale of racial injustice and moral growth.

books.author: George Orwell
books.description: A dystopian novel set in a totalitarian society ruled by Big Brother. Winston Smith struggles against the oppressive regime that controls every aspect of life, even rewriting history.

books.title: The Great Gatsby
books.author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
books.description: A portrait of the Jazz Age in all its decadence and excess. Gatsby's lavish lifestyle and obsessive love for Daisy Buchanan reveal the hollowness of the American Dream.

books.title: Design Patterns
books.author: Erich Gamma, Richard Helm, Ralph Johnson & John Vlissides
books.description: Elements of reusable object-oriented software. Capturing solutions to commonly occurring design problems, this book presents 23 patterns that enable flexible and reusable software architecture.

books.title: Deep Work
books.author: Cal Newport
books.description: Rules for focused success in a distracted world. Cal Newport makes the case that the ability to concentrate without distraction is becoming increasingly valuable and increasingly rare.

books.title: Pride and Prejudice
books.author: Jane Austen
books.description: A sparkling comedy of manners set in Regency England. Elizabeth Bennet and Mr. Darcy navigate misunderstandings, societal expectations, and their own pride in this timeless romance.

books.title: You Don't Know JS
books.author: Kyle Simpson
books.description: A deep dive into the core mechanisms of JavaScript. This series goes beyond syntax to explore scope, closures, the this keyword, object prototypes, types, and async patterns.

books.title: The Alchemist
books.author: Paulo Coelho
books.description: A magical fable about following your dreams. Santiago, a shepherd boy, travels from Spain to the Egyptian desert in search of treasure and discovers the true riches of life along the way.
